rationale: In the hypothesis-development section, the citing paper explicitly uses Dunn and Dahl (2012) as evidence that self-threat can lead consumers to defensively shift blame to the product and evaluate it more negatively. That logic is then carried into the paper's central mechanism for VFR effects among high-BMI consumers. So the citation is doing conceptual and empirical grounding work, not just a passing literature mention.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Dunn and Dahl (2012) as prior support for a self-threat-driven blame-shifting mechanism. It uses that foundation to motivate its own theory that VFR-induced self-image threat can make high-BMI consumers denigrate apparel products.
